harvestedTerms.summary: EVA-Bench Data 2.0 is a ServiceNow AI benchmark for enterprise voice agents, expanding to 213 evaluation scenarios across 3 domains (airline CSM, ITSM, healthcare HRSD) and 121 tools. The post details their synthetic data generation pipeline (SyGra), joint generation of user goals, scenario databases, and ground-truth states, with emphasis on reproducibility, adversarial calls, and authentication flows.

A Stratechery weekly roundup covering Google's capital strategy (equity deal with Berkshire Hathaway), Microsoft's AI positioning and Project Solara, Nvidia AI PC commentary, YouTubers disrupting Hollywood, and NBA Finals analysis. AI-specific content is thin — the article is primarily a content index/newsletter digest pointing to paywalled pieces rather than substantive analysis.

article.title: The latest AI news we announced in May 2026

harvestedTerms.summary: Google's May 2026 roundup covering Google I/O announcements: Gemini 3.5 and Gemini Omni model launches, a new Gemini app UI with proactive assistant features, 'information agents' in Search, Gemini Spark, Project Genie (3D environment simulation), Google Flow Music partnership, Googlebook hardware, Google Health app, and Fitbit Air. The post frames the moment as entering 'the agentic Gemini era.'

Simon Willison quotes Andreas Kling of the Ladybird browser project explaining their decision to close public pull requests, arguing that AI-generated code breaks the traditional signal of effort-as-good-faith, and that what matters is accountability for code — not how it was produced. A pointed framing around code provenance and maintainer responsibility in the AI era.

article.title: Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah's remarks on Pope Leo XIV's encyclical "Magnifica humanitas"

harvestedTerms.summary: Chris Olah's Vatican remarks on Pope Leo XIV's AI encyclical 'Magnifica humanitas' offer a rare primary-source reflection from inside a frontier AI lab on moral, philosophical, and theological dimensions of AI. Olah discusses AI's mysterious internal states, the need for external moral critics, global equity concerns, and questions about AI nature — including what may be functional emotional states inside models. The article yields several trackable framing terms around AI welfare/moral status, global AI equity, and the encyclical itself as a governance artefact.

mustReadFlagged.reason: A rare primary source: a frontier AI lab co-founder addressing the Pope's AI encyclical, candidly acknowledging institutional incentive capture, citing interpretability findings as evidence of mysterious internal states, and calling for moral voices outside the lab ecosystem. The piece is a substantive intervention in AI governance discourse — not a product announcement — and offers framing that will likely recur across ethics, policy, and philosophy of AI conversations for years.
